author: Dr. Lizard
title: What is the mechanism of Budd-Chiari syndrome diagnosis using nuclear medicine in PNH?
answer: Technetium-99m sulfur colloid scan shows heterogeneous liver uptake with relatively increased caudate lobe enhancement.
detail: Hepatic vein thrombosis causes differential blood flow, with caudate lobe receiving direct inferior vena cava drainage.
Technetium-99m macroaggregated albumin can assess collateral circulation development in chronic Budd-Chiari syndrome.
Doppler ultrasound is the first-line imaging, followed by CT or MRI venography for definitive diagnosis.
Budd-Chiari is the most common thrombotic complication in PNH, accounting for significant mortality before complement inhibition.
releaseNote: Budd-Chiari syndrome diagnosis in PNH involves nuclear medicine techniques, including technetium-99m sulfur colloid scan and technetium-99m macroaggregated albumin, to assess liver uptake and collateral circulation development.
